name: Remote Sensing Minor

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The course pool is topically excellent and does build in depth (SET 203 Intro to Remote Sensing -> SET 303 Photogrammetry -> SET 423 Remote Sensing of the Environment and SET 433 RS Digital Image Processing, both depth 5), complemented by raster/vector GIS (SET 220/320), reality capture (SET 405), drone science (ENGR 423) and IoT/microcontrollers (ENGR 330). However, requirementGroups is 0: the minor's plan of study, required-versus-elective structure and course-selection rules are not represented in the data, so ordered progression cannot be confirmed from the catalog page.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the minor's structure explicitly (required core courses, elective menu, and the number of courses/credits needed) so the coherent topical pool becomes a legible, ordered requirement set.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: The prerequisite graph is healthy (deepest chain 6) and the remote-sensing courses have sensible dependencies (SET 423 and SET 433 both require SET 303 Photogrammetry). Only one dangling reference (GER 300) appears. A few prerequisite strings carry parser noise ('Prerequisites or', 'Pre or', 'Courses include ...').
dimensions.suggestion: Clean the fragmented prerequisite strings on SET 203 and SET 220 and resolve the single GER 300 placeholder.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No stated minor-level learning outcomes or purpose statement appear in the data. Even for a minor, a short outcomes statement is expected to communicate what completers can do.
dimensions.suggestion: Add explicit minor learning outcomes (e.g., acquire and interpret multispectral imagery, perform image classification, integrate GIS and RS products).

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: creditReconcileDelta and summedTermCredits are null and requirementGroups is 0. For a minor this is a data-consistency caveat rather than a defect in a full plan of study, but as a result the catalog data does not expose the minor's required credit count or the balance between core RS courses and electives.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the minor's total required credits and the required/elective split so a reader can see the balance; the null reconcile values are expected for a minor and need only a stated credit total.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The minor is represented only as a flat course pool with no requirement groups, no credit total and no indication of which of the 34 listed courses are required versus optional, so a catalog reader cannot determine how to complete the minor. Individual course descriptions are rich, but the page-level structure is essentially missing.
dimensions.suggestion: Render the minor as an explicit requirement structure (core courses + choose-N electives + credit total), which is the single highest-impact fix for this page.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: The pool is strongly current: dedicated remote-sensing courses (SET 203, SET 423, SET 433), raster and vector GIS (SET 220/320), reality capture/point clouds (SET 405), drone science (ENGR 423), IoT (ENGR 330) and robotics (ENGR 424). The gaps are machine-learning-based image analysis and cloud earth-observation platforms rather than fundamentals.
dimensions.suggestion: Modernize SET 433 to include machine-learning/deep-learning image classification and a Google Earth Engine / cloud-processing component.

descriptionQuality.justification: All 34 sampled courses have descriptions and the remote-sensing entries are specific (SET 433 on digital image analysis from airborne/spaceborne instruments; SET 423 on RS applications in natural resources with hands-on real-world work; SET 405 on 3D point-cloud reality capture). A few descriptions begin with stray prerequisite fragments.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisites are mostly present and sensible (SET 423/433 <- SET 303), but several strings are visibly mangled by parsing ('Prerequisites or' on SET 203, 'Pre or' and 'Courses include CS 100 or ...' on SET 220/320), which reduces clarity, and the minor's overall selection rules are not shown.

missingTopics.topic: Machine learning / deep learning for image classification
missingTopics.rationale: SET 433 Digital Image Processing emphasizes classical methods; CNN-based land-cover classification is now standard in remote sensing.

missingTopics.topic: Cloud earth-observation processing (Google Earth Engine, cloud-native rasters)
missingTopics.rationale: SET 220/423 are desktop-oriented; large-scale open-imagery pipelines (Sentinel/Landsat on the cloud) are absent.
SET 423/433 emphasize optical imagery; active radar and hyperspectral sensing are underrepresented.
SET 405 reality capture introduces point clouds but airborne LiDAR processing for RS applications is not covered.
No Python or Earth Engine API scripting course tailored to RS appears in the pool, limiting reproducible-analysis skills.

emergingTrends.trend: Geospatial AI / deep learning for earth observation
emergingTrends.rationale: Automated feature extraction and classification are reshaping RS and would modernize SET 433.
Google Earth Engine and cloud-hosted Sentinel/Landsat archives are becoming the default analysis environment.
Combining ENGR 423 Drone Science with SET 203 enables low-cost, high-resolution multispectral monitoring.
RS is central to change detection and precision-ag applications that SET 423 could target.
Fusing heterogeneous sensors is a growing methodological frontier the minor could introduce.

topSuggestions: Publish the minor's required-course list, elective menu and credit total (currently no requirement structure is represented in the catalog data).
Add machine-learning/deep-learning image classification and a Python/Earth Engine scripting component (modernize SET 433).
Add cloud geospatial processing and SAR/hyperspectral coverage.
Clean the fragmented prerequisite strings on SET 203/220/320 and resolve the GER 300 placeholder.
Leverage ENGR 423 Drone Science and SET 203 for a UAS-multispectral capstone experience.
